Create Website

This page explains what marque new actually gives you and what you should do with it next.

Quickstart gets the site running. This page is about understanding the scaffold you now own.

Scaffold a site

Create and run a new site

Start with the default starter:

sh
marque new my-site
cd my-site
marque serve .

If you already know the shell you want, pick a layout and theme up front:

sh
marque new my-site --layout topnav --theme rustique

What the starter contains

my-site/
├── directives/   # project-specific directives loaded on build and serve
├── layouts/      # shared HTML shell and layout CSS
├── pages/        # source pages written in .mq
├── static/       # copied as-is into the generated site
├── themes/       # visual styling
├── marque.toml   # site-wide defaults
└── navigation.mq # navigation labels, order, and grouping

The generated dist/ folder appears later, once you run marque build or marque serve.

Files in static/ are copied to the site root. From any .mq page, link them with paths such as static/logo.png or static/images/hero.png.

The starter is meant to be edited directly. Replace the example pages, keep what helps, and delete what does not.

Start with these files

marque.toml

This is where the site-wide defaults live: title, description, repo link, layout, theme, width, and alignment.

It answers the question, "what should happen everywhere unless a page says otherwise?"

navigation.mq

This file controls navigation labels, ordering, grouping, and nesting.

It answers the question, "how should the menu actually look?"

pages/

This is your source content. Each .mq file becomes a page in the generated site.

mq
+++
title = My Page
nav = my-page
+++

# My Page

directives/, themes/, and layouts/

These folders are where the starter stops being generic.

  • directives/: custom authoring blocks and inline helpers
  • themes/: colours, type, spacing, and component styling
  • layouts/: shared page shell and navigation placement

pages/404.mq

This is the fallback page for missing routes. Keep it around, but do not treat it like a configuration file.

What to edit first

Open marque.toml and set the site title, layout, theme, and default alignment.

Edit navigation.mq so the navigation reflects the site you actually want to build.

Replace or remove the starter pages in pages/.

Touch themes/, layouts/, or directives/ only once the content structure is clear.

Mental model

Source vs generated output

Edit source files in pages/, themes/, layouts/, directives/, marque.toml, and navigation.mq.

Treat dist/ as disposable output.

Defaults vs overrides

Put shared decisions in marque.toml. Put one-off decisions in page frontmatter. Put menu decisions in navigation.mq.

Pages vs navigation

A page can exist in pages/ without appearing in navigation.

If it should show up in the menu, it needs an entry in navigation.mq.

Structure before polish

Get the content, routing, and navigation right first.

Once those feel solid, move on to themes, layouts, and custom directives.

Where to go next

Learn the config

Once the scaffold makes sense, the next stop is the configuration docs.

Open configuration Navigation Frontmatter

Learn the authoring layer

When markdown stops being enough, move on to directives and themes.

Directives Themes
